message:
fix(resources): force-release reservations whose holder agent has been pruned

`file_reservations` walks each project's reservations and decides
which can be auto-released based on holder liveness signals
(workspace availability, recent mail activity, last-active timestamp).
Previously the path that handled "holder not seen recently" only
ran when the workspace tempdir was still on disk:

```rust
if !workspace_available {
    continue;
}
```

When the holder agent's row had been pruned from the agents table
entirely (DB compaction, failed registration, manual cleanup), there
was no `agent_last_active` to compare against and `agent_inactive`
defaulted to `true` — but `workspace_available` would also be false
because the agent's identity file no longer existed. The combination
hit the `continue` above and the reservation became a permanent
phantom blocker until `expires_ts` elapsed (which can be hours away).

Add a dedicated orphan-release branch that runs BEFORE the workspace
gate:

```rust
let agent_missing = agent_last_active.is_none();
// ...
if agent_missing && !recent_mail {
    let updated = force_release_reservation(..., Some(row.expires_ts))?;
    if updated > 0 { released_ids.push(id); }
    continue;
}
```

Rationale: when the holder's agent row is gone, there is no live
owner to coordinate with — fs/git signals can't tell us anything
useful about a non-existent owner, so we must release regardless of
workspace availability. The `!recent_mail` guard preserves the
existing safety check for an agent that's writing mail under a
since-renamed identity (rare but possible).

The non-orphan path (`agent_missing == false`, holder is just
inactive) still requires `workspace_available` to confirm the
agent is reachable before releasing.

## Test workspace simplification

Two tests in `reservations.rs` and `resources.rs` were creating a
real `tempfile::tempdir()` workspace just to use its path as a
`project_key`. Neither test actually exercised workspace contents,
and the tempdir tear-down was racing against the test's own
`force_release` / orphan-release paths under high parallelism (the
workspace would be cleaned up between the test's setup and its
assertion, masking the assertion's intent).

Switch both to a deterministic `/tmp/<test-name>-<unique-suffix>`
project_key. The path doesn't have to exist on disk — `project_key`
is a string identifier in the agents table, not a file path the
reservation system reads back. This removes the cleanup race and
makes the failure mode (assertion failure vs. tempdir race) clear.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
